text: In October 2006, the American Council on Education (ACE) released an issue brief titled “Students on the Move: The Future of International Students in the United States.” This issue brief synthesizes the current state of knowledge about the international student population in the United States and provides an analysis of the U.S. competitiveness in the global market of studying abroad. While the United States is still the country of choice among international students, current trends among both sending and host countries threaten U.S. competitiveness in this area. The U.S. position as the world’s leading destination for international students should not be taken for granted.
